summary:
Operation Overlord was the Allied invasion of German-occupied Western Europe, launched on June 6, 1944 — D-Day — with the largest amphibious assault in history. Over 156,000 American, British, Canadian, and other Allied troops landed on five beaches along a 80-kilometer stretch of the Normandy coast in northern France, supported by 13,000 aircraft and nearly 7,000 naval vessels. The operation had been planned for over a year and was preceded by an elaborate deception campaign, Operation Bodyguard, designed to mislead the Germans into believing the main assault would come at Pas-de-Calais.

The initial landings were costly, particularly at Omaha Beach where American forces suffered severe casualties from determined German defenses. However, by nightfall all five beaches were secured and the Allied forces had established a foothold on the continent. The success of the landings proved decisive: within weeks, a massive Allied force was ashore in Normandy, beginning the campaign that would eventually liberate France and drive through Western Europe toward Germany.

Operation Overlord, combined with the Soviet Union's simultaneous summer offensive, Bagration, created a two-front military crisis for Germany from which it could not recover. The [[event:2659|D-Day landings]] opened the Western Front that had been absent since the fall of France in 1940, fulfilling a Soviet request that had strained Allied relations for years. The operation demonstrated the extraordinary planning, logistical, and industrial capacity that Allied air and sea power had accumulated, and its success is widely regarded as the turning point in the liberation of Western Europe.
